tables.title: Other Fees
rows: Gross Sales includes all invoiced sales incl. subcontractor fees. Some older agreements have lower rates.
Population × per-capita rate × 4%. Rate escalates: $0 (partial yr), $1 (yr1), $2 (yr2), $3 (yr3), $3.50 (yr4), $4 (yr5), $4.50 (yr6), $5 (yr7), $5.50 (yr8), $6.00 (yr9+). CPI-adjusted.
Only to extent actual royalties fall below minimum
0.5% of GS (1% for resales) until $25,000 for first 500K pop + $2,000 per additional 100K

Paid to Completion Services, Inc. Non-refundable.

Not currently required but PDRI reserves right to implement

New Owner: $22,000 (Program B); Resale Owner: $15,000 (Program A); GM only: $8,000

table_notes: All fees nonrefundable and uniformly imposed unless otherwise stated.
Late payment assessed at 1.5% per month.
Note 1: Guarantee Fund, Computer Software Support, SMP, and Indemnity Fund voted into Operations Manual by Councils.
Note 2: RMS fee $525/month primary, $300/month secondary (common ownership).
Note 3: Resale fee 2.5% of sale price, capped at $40,000. Transfer fee $10K majority / $5K minority. Optional lead gen/broker fees $30K-$50K.
Note 4: Optional programs (Commercial, FAST) require additional training/admin fees. Admin fees deducted from invoice; royalty on net.
tables.provenance: Extracted from Item 6 fee table and Notes, PDF pages 16-23

tables.title: Estimated Initial Investment
table_notes: Based on 500,000 population base territory.
Higher range contemplates higher cost-of-living states (e.g., California) or large urban markets.
Note 1: VetFran 25% discount on franchise fee available.
Note 2: No buildout required. Typical office: strip center, light industrial (3K office + 8K industrial). Typical rent $5K-$15K/month.
Note 3: No required minimum marketing spend, but strongly recommended.
Note 4: Computer system $13K-$25K (server, 2 PCs, printer, software). Fax, copier, scanner, internet required.
Note 5: Full-size extended van required. Purchase $50K-$91K incl. decals/racks. Optional vehicle mount $15K-$30K.
Note 6: Start-up equipment from multiple PDRI-arranged suppliers. May finance with 10% down.
Note 7: Three-week training travel: airfare to Jacksonville, car rental, hotel, meals. $5K-$7.5K.
Note 8: Insurance $20.5K-$63.5K. CA/NY higher. Begin search early due to potential delays.
Note 9: Licensing varies by state/locality. Examinations and significant fees may apply.
Note 10: CPA setup $1K-$2K. May be waived as new client incentive.
Note 11: Minimum working capital $500K recommended. After Year 1: 10% of prior year sales or $500K, whichever is greater.
Note 12: Security deposit typically equals one month rent.
tables.provenance: Extracted from Item 7 table and notes, PDF pages 24-28

rows: C. Site development and other pre-opening requirements

tables.title: Owner's Training Program (2025)
rows: Estimating, Project Management, Job Management (RMS) and General Operations
IT Systems and Loss Document Process Training
table_notes: JCA Training: 16 hours classroom + 6 hours online + 16 hours onsite during Launch Week.
Training is mandatory for all new franchisees including resales.
Program offered approximately 4 times per year.

Consists of: (i) up to 3 weeks classroom in Jacksonville, (ii) 1 week field launch at franchise, (iii) 1 week mentor at existing franchise, (iv) up to 3 weeks online pre-training, (v) optional 3-day advanced class ~6 months later.
tables.provenance: Extracted from Item 11 training tables, PDF pages 34-35

table_notes: Advertising funds not used to solicit new franchisees.
Unspent funds accrued for subsequent fiscal years.
SMP governed by General Council with NEC oversight; managed by PDRI.

tables.title: Renewal, Termination, Transfer, and Dispute Resolution
rows: 90-180 day notice; sign then-current FA (may have materially different terms); equipment to current standards; compliance; minimum sales (greater of national median or $6.00 × population) for each of prior 2 years; pay $5,000 renewal fee; execute within 90 days
e. Termination by franchisor without cause
15 days to cure (unless state law requires longer): nonpayment, supplier payment failure, Operations Manual non-compliance, license/permit failures
15-day cure: nonpayment, supplier issues, Operations Manual, licenses/permits

No cure: failure to begin operations timely; material/repeated GS misrepresentation; insolvency; unapproved transfer; felony; 3+ defaults in 12 months; abandonment (incl. 2+ months no sales reported); confidential info disclosure; non-renewal
De-identification; payment of sums due; transfer phone/domain; return materials
No restriction; assignee must be willing/able to assume obligations
Transfer of contract, assets, or any ownership change
PDRI has right to approve all transfers; same criteria as new franchisees
Qualified transferee; transfer fee; training; new FA at current terms; equipment upgrade; accounts settled
PDRI has right on same terms as third-party offer
On termination, PDRI can purchase at fair market value
Heir can inherit if approval conditions met
Cannot participate in competing business, disrupt employment, or divert business
2 years within protected territory and other PDRI territories; no contact of former suppliers/customers for competitive purpose
Only FA terms binding; outside representations unenforceable (subject to state law)
Binding arbitration under PDRI internal system (arbitrators are franchisees)
Arbitration; forum selected by Arbitration Committee; may not be litigated (subject to state law)
table_notes: Renewal requires signing then-current FA which may have materially different terms — significant risk.
Non-competition: 2 years post-term within own territory AND other PDRI territories.
Arbitration is internal (franchisee-operated) — unusual structure.
tables.provenance: Extracted from Item 17 table, PDF pages 46-50

tables.title: FS Brands, Inc. Consolidated Balance Sheet (Dec 31, 2024 vs 2023)
table_notes: FS Brands, Inc. is the parent of PDRI — consolidated across all subsidiaries (California Closets, CertaPro, FCI, Pillar to Post, etc.).
No external long-term debt.
Goodwill $137M — significant portion of total assets.
$30M dividend paid in 2024 (first dividend in recent period).
tables.provenance: Extracted from Exhibit B-1, PDF pages 93-94

tables.title: FS Brands, Inc. Consolidated Income Statement (FY2024 vs FY2023)
table_notes: Revenue grew 8.7% YoY ($782M to $850M).
Operating income grew 22.0% YoY ($67.8M to $82.7M).
Operating margin: 9.7% (2024) vs 8.7% (2023).
Effective tax rate: 27.9% (2024) vs 26.0% (2023).
Merchandise sales dominate revenue (75.9% of total) — reflects company-owned operations of other brands.
Management fees to parent: $7.5M (2024).
tables.provenance: Extracted from Exhibit B-1, PDF page 95
